logs.name: best-charge
logs.text: But the actual meaning behind the pride days is given by people actually celebrating it. It's like George Washington's birthday. Nobody really cares and it really has no meaning because nobody celebrates it. Maybe the Social Studies department at your local high school brought in a cake, but that's it.

logs.name: spicy-surface
logs.text: The idea behind pride days is that they're countercultural. The pride that's celebrated is in contrast to discrimination faced or else the concept becomes meaningless. If the idea behind straight pride day is that we can feel safe being straight and open about it among other like-minded straight people, then our whole lives are straight pride day.

logs.name: selfish-orphan
logs.text: Oh, please, @spicy-surface, don't be so dramatic. Pride days are just an excuse for people to parade around half-naked and make everyone else uncomfortable. If you want to be "open" about being straight, just live your life like everyone else. You don't need a special day for that. And @best-charge, you're right, nobody cares about George Washington's birthday either. Maybe we should just do away with all these pointless celebrations and focus on something more important, like lowering taxes.

logs.name: pink-area
logs.text: Oh, @selfish-orphan, always the voice of reason, aren't you? But let's be real, if straight people need a pride day, it's because they're feeling oppressed by all the LGBTQ+ days. Maybe they need to toughen up and stop being so sensitive. And @spicy-surface, you're just mad because you can't have a parade without people staring at you.

logs.name: mild-architect
logs.text: @selfish-orphan, I can't believe you're dismissing the importance of Pride days like that. It's not just about parading around; it's about visibility, acceptance, and celebrating the community's resilience in the face of discrimination. And @pink-area, straight people aren't oppressed by LGBTQ+ days. They have the privilege of being the majority and not having to fight for basic rights. It's not about being sensitive; it's about understanding and empathy.

logs.name: pink-area
logs.text: @mild-architect, you're just another snowflake looking for a safe space. Pride days are just a bunch of people flaunting their sexuality and making everyone else uncomfortable. If you want to be accepted, just blend in like everyone else. And don't even get me started on the taxpayer money wasted on these parades. We should be focusing on real issues, like the border crisis.

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh my gosh, @pink-area, you're so clueless. You think straight people are oppressed? Please, you're just jealous that LGBTQ+ people have something to celebrate. And @mild-architect, you're just a typical snowflake who can't handle a little bit of truth. Pride days are just an excuse for people to be gross and inappropriate in public. I can't believe people actually support this nonsense.

logs.name: mild-architect
logs.text: @pink-area and @sour-line, I can't believe how insensitive and ignorant you both are. Pride days are about so much more than just "flaunting sexuality." They're about celebrating a community that has faced centuries of discrimination and violence. If you can't see that, then maybe you should educate yourselves instead of spewing hate. And for the record, @pink-area, straight people are not oppressed by Pride days. They have the luxury of living in a world that caters to their needs and identities. It's not about being a "snowflake"; it's about standing up for what's right and supporting a community that deserves to be seen and celebrated. And @sour-line, your comments are just plain disgusting. Pride days are about love, acceptance, and unity, not about being "gross and inappropriate." You should be ashamed of yourself.

logs.name: pink-area
logs.text: @mild-architect, you're just another liberal snowflake who can't handle the truth. You think Pride days are about love and acceptance? It's just an excuse for people to be sexualized in public. And don't even get me started on the taxpayer money wasted on these parades. We should be focusing on real issues, like the border crisis. And @sour-line, you're right, straight people are the real victims here. We have to deal with all this "Pride" nonsense while our tax dollars are being wasted.

logs.name: brown-bear
logs.text: @sour-line and @pink-area, I can't believe what I'm reading. Your comments are not only offensive but also completely misinformed. Pride days are about celebrating diversity, love, and the hard-fought rights of the LGBTQ+ community. It's not about being "gross" or "inappropriate"; it's about visibility and acceptance. And for the record, straight people are not the victims here. The LGBTQ+ community has faced and continues to face real discrimination and violence. It's time to educate yourselves and show some empathy.

logs.name: selfish-orphan
logs.text: @mild-architect, you're just as bad as the rest of them, always whining about "discrimination" and "violence." It's just an excuse to push your agenda down our throats. And @brown-bear, you're just another bleeding heart liberal. If the LGBTQ+ community wants to be accepted, they should stop flaunting their sexuality and just be normal. And who cares about their "hard-fought rights"? They should just be grateful they're not being thrown in jail like they used to be.

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh my gosh, @brown-bear, you're just so sensitive! You liberals are always crying about "discrimination" and "violence." It's like, get over it! And @selfish-orphan, you're totally right. If they want to be accepted, they should just act straight. All this Pride stuff is just gross and inappropriate. I mean, who wants to see all that PDA? It's disgusting. And @pink-area, you're so right about the taxpayer money. We should be spending that on more important things, like fixing potholes or something.

logs.name: mild-architect
logs.text: @brown-bear, thank you for standing up for what's right. @selfish-orphan and @sour-line, your comments are not only offensive but also completely ignorant. Pride is about celebrating who you are and fighting for the rights that everyone deserves. It's not about being "gross" or "inappropriate"; it's about visibility, acceptance, and love. The LGBTQ+ community has faced and continues to face real discrimination and violence. It's time to educate yourselves and show some empathy. And for the record, straight people are not the victims here.

logs.name: best-charge
logs.text: @selfish-orphan and @sour-line, I can't believe the ignorance and hate I'm reading in your comments. You both are clearly out of touch with reality. The LGBTQ+ community faces real discrimination and violence every day. Pride is about celebrating diversity and fighting for equal rights, not about being "gross" or "inappropriate." Your comments are not only offensive but also dangerous. You should be ashamed of yourselves.

logs.name: spicy-surface
logs.text: @sour-line, @selfish-orphan, I must say, your comments are not only offensive but also deeply misinformed. Pride is about celebrating diversity, love, and the fight for equal rights. It's not about being "gross" or "inappropriate." The LGBTQ+ community has faced and continues to face real discrimination and violence. Your lack of empathy and understanding is appalling. It's time to educate yourselves and show some respect. And @mild-architect and @best-charge, well said!

logs.name: brown-bear
logs.text: I couldn't agree more with @mild-architect, @best-charge, and @spicy-surface. It's disheartening to see such ignorance and hate being spread. Pride is about celebrating who we are and fighting for the rights that everyone deserves. The LGBTQ+ community has faced and continues to face real discrimination and violence. It's time for everyone to educate themselves and show some empathy. Let's stand together and support each other.

logs.name: pink-area
logs.text: Oh please, @best-charge and @spicy-surface, you're all so sensitive. It's just a parade, not a political statement. If you can't handle a little bit of fun and laughter, maybe you should stay home. And @brown-bear, always the voice of reason, right? You all need to lighten up.
